mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, invalidating the earnings recession channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with accelerating retail sales indicate strong consumer demand outpacing monetary tightening, producing positive equity returns as earnings growth validates higher discount rates.
failure_condition: The interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or joint signal IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving supply-side capacity utilization and corporate output growth, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5.0% annually, indicating inflation overrides supply improvements.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Monthly payroll changes show zero correlation with 20-day forward returns across all business cycle phases, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches 260.0, entrenched inflation forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of periods following CPIAUCSL crossing above 260.0, contradicting valuation compression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate a synchronized recovery in consumer confidence and labor market strength, jointly driving positive equity returns as household formation boosts durable goods demand.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series in predictive accuracy, with interaction term IC below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth signals contracting aggregate demand and weakening corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns during late-cycle economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth periods consistently yield positive 60-day equity returns, contradicting earnings recession dynamics and invalidating the demand channel.
